title: Fix SyntaxError: Cannot declare a let variable twice
description:
# Fix Syntax Errors in refactored JS

## 📝 User Story / Description
The refactoring process introduced two syntax errors when modifying object properties (`let state.menuOpen` and `const state.segHeight`). These need to be fixed to restore functionality.

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fix syntax error `let state.menuOpen` in `visualizer/main.js`
- [ ] Fix syntax error `const state.segHeight` in `visualizer/rendering.js`
- [ ] Visualizer runs without JS syntax errors

## 🛠️ Technical Notes
- Replace `let state.menuOpen = false;` with `state.menuOpen = false;`
- Replace `const state.segHeight = ...` with `state.segHeight = ...`

## 🛡️ Rigor & Verification Boundary
- [ ] Check Chrome DevTools console to ensure no errors
- [ ] `nomos verify` passes cleanly

- **Quality Debt Exemptions:**
  - `complexity_limit: false`
  - `doc_drift: false`
  - `dry_candidate: false`
